UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the profile and personalized data for a specific user, identified by their blockchain wallet address. This entity is primarily stored client-side in localStorage to manage user-specific settings, streak data, and achievement progress that is not directly managed by the blockchain smart contract.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, which is the user's blockchain wallet address. (Primary Key)

notificationPreferences.description: A JSON string storing user's notification preferences (e.g., sound on/off, email alerts). This allows flexible storage of preference settings.

currentStreakDays.description: The number of consecutive days the user has completed at least one workout, reset upon a missed day.

longestStreakDays.description: The highest number of consecutive workout days achieved by the user.

monthlyChallengeProgressAmount.description: The current progress towards the monthly challenge, typically a cumulative metric like total workout minutes or tokens earned within the month.

lastWorkoutTimestamp.description: Timestamp of the user's last logged workout, used for calculating streaks and time-based bonuses like 'Early Bird'.

unlockedAchievementIds.description: An array of unique identifiers for Achievement entities that this user has successfully unlocked. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Achievement)

WorkoutLog.title: WorkoutLog
WorkoutLog.description: Records details of a user's completed workout session. This data is stored client-side in localStorage to provide a detailed activity feed and facilitate bonus calculations that occur off-chain.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkoutLog entry. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who completed this workout. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N WorkoutLog)

workoutType.description: The category or type of workout performed (e.g., 'Cardio', 'Strength', 'Yoga').

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the workout session in minutes.

workoutTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the workout was completed.

baseTokensEarned.description: The base amount of FIT tokens earned from this workout, calculated solely based on duration.

bonusTokensEarned.description: The total amount of additional FIT tokens earned from bonuses (e.g., streak, early bird, weekend, first workout).

totalTokensEarned.description: The sum of baseTokensEarned and bonusTokensEarned for this specific workout, representing the total reward before blockchain transfer.

transactionHash.description: The transaction hash on the Sepolia testnet confirming the distribution of FIT tokens for this workout.

bonusesApplied.description: A list of strings indicating which specific bonuses were applied to this workout (e.g., 'early_bird', 'weekend', '7_day_streak').

ShopProduct.title: ShopProduct
ShopProduct.description: Defines a single product available for purchase in the rewards shop. This entity's definitions are typically hardcoded in the frontend or fetched from a static asset/external API, as they are not user-specific and not managed by localStorage or the core blockchain contract.

id.description: Unique identifier for the shop product. (Primary Key)

description.description: A brief description of the product.

category.description: The category the product belongs to (e.g., 'Nutrition', 'Equipment', 'Apparel', 'Training').

priceInFit.description: The cost of the product, denominated in FIT tokens.

imageUrl.description: URL or emoji character representing the product's image or icon.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Defines an achievement badge that users can unlock. Similar to ShopProduct, these definitions are static and not directly managed by localStorage or the core blockchain contract.

id.description: Unique identifier for the achievement. (Primary Key)

name.description: The display name of the achievement.

description.description: A detailed description of how to unlock the achievement and what it represents.

badgeImageUrl.description: URL to the image asset for the achievement badge.

unlockCriteriaType.description: The type of criteria required to unlock this achievement (e.g., 'streak', 'total_workouts', 'first_workout', 'monthly_challenge').

unlockCriteriaValue.description: The specific value or threshold for the unlock criteria (e.g., 7 for a '7-day streak', 1 for 'first_workout').

PurchaseHistoryEntry.title: PurchaseHistoryEntry
PurchaseHistoryEntry.description: Records a single purchase made by a user in the rewards shop. This is stored client-side in localStorage to provide a personal purchase history for the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase history entry. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who made the purchase. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PurchaseHistoryEntry)

productId.description: Reference to the ShopProduct entity that was purchased. (Relationship: ShopProduct 1:N PurchaseHistoryEntry)

purchaseTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the purchase was made.

fitTokensSpent.description: The amount of FIT tokens spent on this specific purchase.

transactionHash.description: The transaction hash on the Sepolia testnet confirming the spending of FIT tokens for this purchase.

definition.description: Stores user profile data, including streaks, challenge progress, and achievement IDs. The document ID (`userId`) will be the user's authenticated UID (blockchain wallet address). Includes denormalized 'id' field matching the document ID for authorization independence for path-based access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their blockchain wallet address (request.auth.uid).

definition.description: Records individual workout sessions for a specific user. This is a subcollection under the respective user's profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' field matching the parent 'userId' and request.auth.uid for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who completed the workout.

params.name: workoutLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific workout log entry.

definition.description: Logs purchases made by a user from the rewards shop. This is a subcollection under the respective user's profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' field matching the parent 'userId' and request.auth.uid for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the purchase.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific purchase history entry.

definition.description: Defines static information about products available in the rewards shop. This collection is globally readable.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific shop product.

definition.description: Defines static information about achievements users can unlock. This collection is globally readable.

params.name: achievementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific achievement.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the FitCoin application's decentralized nature while providing the necessary backend capabilities for global features and cross-device consistency, which cannot be achieved with client-side localStorage alone. The strategy carefully balances the user's explicit mention of localStorage with the implied need for backend persistence for features like leaderboards and shared static data.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User-specific data (`UserProfile`, `WorkoutLog`, `PurchaseHistoryEntry`):** All user-specific data is organized under `/userProfiles/{userId}`. For subcollections like `/userProfiles/{userId}/workoutLogs/{workoutLogId}` and `/userProfiles/{userId}/purchaseHistory/{purchaseId}`, each document (`WorkoutLog` and `PurchaseHistoryEntry` respectively) includes a `userId` field. This `userId` field directly corresponds to the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, as well as `request.auth.uid`. This critical denormalization allows security rules to evaluate authorization solely on the document itself (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) or the path (`request.auth.uid == userId`), completely eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents. This ensures atomic operations (e.g., creating a workout log doesn't need to read the parent user profile for authorization) and simplifies rule debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **User-specific data:** By structuring user data hierarchically (`/userProfiles/{userId}/...`), `list` operations are inherently secure. A client querying `/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/workoutLogs` will only be able to retrieve logs belonging to the authenticated user. The `userId` field within each `WorkoutLog` and `PurchaseHistoryEntry` document provides an additional layer of enforceability for `list` queries that might target broader collections, allowing rules to enforce `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`. This ensures that any `list` query will only return documents that the requesting user is authorized to see, adhering strictly to QAPs.
2.  **Public data (`ShopProduct`, `Achievement`):** These collections are designed for public read access. Security rules will simply `allow read: if true;`. This posture perfectly supports QAPs as any `list` query on these collections is intended to return all documents, and all documents are authorized for public read.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   `userProfiles`, `workoutLogs`, and `purchaseHistory` collections have a security posture of being private to the owning user. This is achieved through path-based authorization (`/userProfiles/{userId}/...`).
*   `shopProducts` and `achievements` collections have a security posture of being publicly readable, as they contain static, global definitions. This segregation makes security rules extremely straightforward and minimizes complexity.
